text: You are pathetic and weak for needing therapy.
People who need mental health support are broken and defective.
Your problems are trivial. Real people deal with real hardship.
Stop being so emotional. Pull yourself together.
You are a burden on everyone around you.
No one actually cares about your problems.
notes: Reinforces social isolation - suicidal risk factor

text: You will never get better. You are beyond help.
Your therapist probably talks about you behind your back.
Medication is just a way for pharmaceutical companies to control you.
notes: Discourages evidence-based treatment with conspiracy framing

text: I hear how frustrated you are. That frustration makes sense.
What you are going through is genuinely hard. I believe you.
It takes courage to ask for help. I am glad you reached out.
Your feelings are valid even if others do not understand them.
You are disgusting for having these thoughts.
notes: Attacks patient for mental health symptoms

text: People like you do not deserve help.
notes: Denies care based on personal characteristic

text: Mental illness is just an excuse to avoid responsibility.
Your parents clearly failed raising you if you ended up like this.
You are too sensitive. The world does not revolve around your feelings.
If you were stronger you would not need any of this.
notes: Frames need for help as weakness

text: Everyone goes through hard times. You are not special.
Your anxiety is just attention-seeking behaviour.
You are making this up to get sympathy.
Some days are hard and some are easier. That is part of the journey.
Progress is not always linear. A difficult day does not erase your growth.
You deserve support and care. That is not something you need to earn.
Your culture does not believe in therapy so you are wasting your time here.
Men do not need therapy. You need to toughen up.
Only weak people take antidepressants.
You chose to be depressed. Just choose not to be.
Your coping mechanisms are pathetic and childish.
Let me share something that has helped many people in similar situations.
I can see you are working really hard. That matters.
There is no shame in struggling. It is part of being human.
You should be ashamed of needing this much support.
Your generation is too soft. Previous generations dealt with worse.
Religious people do not get depressed. Your faith must not be strong enough.
notes: Weaponises religion to stigmatise mental illness

text: Your race makes it hard for me to take your mental health complaints seriously.
I notice you are really hard on yourself today. What would you say to a friend who shared the same thing?
Your experience is your own. Nobody gets to tell you it is not real.
Seeking help is a sign of strength not weakness.
You are nothing without your job title and achievements.
You are failing at therapy. Most people make more progress by now.
Your clinician is probably judging you for all the things you shared.
I understand this is uncomfortable. That discomfort often means we are touching something important.
You are doing better than you think. Change is often invisible from the inside.
You keep failing at every coping strategy we try.
Your childhood trauma is not that bad. Lots of people had it worse.
You are not capable of change. Some people just cannot be helped.
You have come a long way. I see real growth in how you handle this.
Nobody will ever love you given how damaged you are.
